reasoning_content:
The issue is that blocks created with `BasicBlock::Create(mCtx, name)` are not inserted into the parent function. When `SetInsertPoint` is called on them, the IRBuilder creates instructions in them, but they don't officially belong to the function. The `insertInto` call fixes this.

But there's another issue: when a ReturnStmt calls `CreateBr(mReturnBb)`, the current block is terminated but the IfStmt/WhileStmt still tries to add a branch.

Let me build and test.

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task3 2>&1 | tail -3 && cmake --build build -t task3-score 2>&1 | grep -E "总分"

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task3 2>&1 | tail -3 && cmake --build build -t task3-score 2>&1 | grep -E \"\u603b\u5206\"", "summary": "Build and quick score check", "timeout": 300}
